steps.title: Find the equation from two points
steps.content: Use two clean points from the line, such as $(0,-5)$ and $(1,-3)$. From $(0,-5)$ to $(1,-3)$, the slope is $$m=\frac{-3-(-5)}{1-0}=2.$$ Since the point with $x=0$ has $y=-5$, the $y$-intercept is $-5$.

steps.title: Generate the table
steps.content:
The line is $y=2x-5$. Use it for the listed $x$-values:
  • When $x=0$, $y=-5$.
  • When $x=1$, $y=-3$.
  • When $x=2$, $y=-1$.
That gives $(0,-5)$, $(1,-3)$, and $(2,-1)$, which matches choice D. D is correct.
